codeQualityIssues.description: Rng struct (xorshift64) is duplicated verbatim across stress_buddy_coalescing.rs, stress_alloc_fragmentation.rs, stress_sched_ctx_concurrent.rs, and scheduler_state.rs. This is acceptable given the self-contained test file architecture, but a shared test utility module could reduce duplication if the pattern continues to spread.

Section 9 (checkerboard) re-allocates even pages to restore fully-allocated state before freeing all. The re-allocated pages may not be in the same positions as the originals — the test handles this correctly by freeing odd_pages + re_even (all distinct allocations), but a comment clarifying this intent would aid readability.

Test 5 (mixed_operations_random) phase 3 uses raw indexing into ctx_ids for handle closure. If a context was already freed by a prior phase's operations, dec_ref returns None (slot empty). The code doesn't check the return value, which is fine since the handle is being closed (the slot may have been freed by thread exits if the handle was already closed... but wait, these handles haven't been closed yet). No actual bug — the logic is correct because handles_to_close iterates 0..N from ctx_ids which were all created fresh this round and their handle refs haven't been released yet.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The kernel-auditor skill's stress test guidance says 'Use seeded RNG (let mut rng = StdRng::seed_from_u64(42);)' but the project has no `rand` crate dependency. All existing stress tests use a custom xorshift64 Rng struct instead. The skill should reference the project's actual pattern: 'Use a seeded deterministic PRNG (xorshift64 Rng struct, see existing stress test files for the pattern).'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md adversarial fuzzing section references StdRng. Cargo.toml has no rand dependency. 4 test files use custom Rng struct: stress_buddy_coalescing.rs:107, stress_alloc_fragmentation.rs:126, scheduler_state.rs:494, stress_sched_ctx_concurrent.rs:22.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges that the skill should document the page_allocator global static behavior: init() ADDS pages to existing state, so all buddy tests must be in ONE test function or carefully drain state between tests. This is a real gotcha — the worker hit it during development and had to restructure from multiple test functions to a single mega-test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0] and stress_buddy_coalescing.rs header comment (lines 6-10).

summary: Both test files are well-implemented and thoroughly cover the feature requirements. The buddy coalescing test (stress_buddy_coalescing.rs, 761 lines) exercises all 15 merge paths in a single test function with clear section documentation, seeded RNG for determinism, corruption detection in interleaved sections, and baseline free_count verification after every section. The scheduling context concurrent test (stress_sched_ctx_concurrent.rs, 912 lines) faithfully models the kernel's ref_count protocol across 8 tests covering bind/close/exit ordering, borrow/return paths, exit-while-borrowing, slot reuse, precise ref_count tracking, and 500-op interleaved operations — all with seeded RNG and leak verification. Code follows established project patterns (stub modules, #[path] imports, xorshift64 RNG, single-threaded test constraint). No blocking issues found. Two minor skill documentation gaps noted.
